title: Package Layout, Pyproject Metadata, Entry Points, and Reproducible Builds · Transfer Build
summary: An independent archive team receives a different authorized corpus, platform, language mix, file size, report consumer, terminal width, error policy, and delivery constraint and transfers package layout, pyproject metadata, entry points, and reproducible builds into a changed installable reproducible Bookbot package. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of installable reproducible Bookbot package with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.
Declare canonical name, version policy, Python requirement, description, license, authorship, classifiers, URLs, and build system in pyproject.toml.
Support python -m execution and an installed console script that call the same testable main boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Src Layout
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 4275 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to build inspected wheel and source artifacts from an identified revision and test a fresh installation; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Src Layout.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery. steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Installed Smoke
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-installed-smoke-1dpby7" that uses case 8070 to build inspected wheel and source artifacts from an identified revision and test a fresh installation. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-installed-smoke-1dpby7
# Competency: bookbot-installed-smoke.
# Case 8070: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_installed_smoke_1dpby7(metadata_fields: int = 9, artifact_count: int = 2, clean_revision: bool = True, contents_inspected: bool = True, fresh_install_passed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1dpby7 = "1dpby7-7ybpd1-8070"
    assert evidence_variant_1dpby7
    evidence_axes_1dpby7 = (
        "corpus1dpby7",
        "policy7ybpd1",
        "revision8070",
        "command1dpby78070",
        "report7ybpd18070",
        "test80701dpby7",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1dpby7)) == 6
    if metadata_fields < 6 or artifact_count < 2:
        return False, artifact_count, "package-shape"
    if not clean_revision:
        return False, artifact_count, "dirty-build"
    if not contents_inspected:
        return False, artifact_count, "unknown-contents"
    if not fresh_install_passed:
        return False, artifact_count, "install-failure"
    assert artifact_count >= 2
    return True, metadata_fields, "installable-artifacts"
